constraints: Keep structure shallow (2–3 levels).
Use one Inbox/To Sort location for all new items; process often.
Automate what you can (photo upload, backups, smart rules).
Archive rarely-used items instead of over-organizing.
System must be quick to use.
Maintainable with minimal effort.
No more than six top-level folders.
Filenames must begin with an ISO date (YYYY-MM-DD) to ensure chronological sorting.
Prefer free or built-in tools (OS file manager + free cloud accounts) over paid specialized software.
Adopt a simple Home folder layout with Inbox, Documents (Finance/Personal/Work), Photos, Receipts, Archive.
Choose a naming convention: YYYY-MM-DD_description or Short descriptors; apply consistently.
Auto-upload photos from phone to Home/Photos and use cloud sync as primary storage with backups.
OS automation: macOS (Smart Folders + Hazel/Shortcuts) or Windows (Power Automate); cross-platform: forward receipts into Receipts via Zapier/IFTTT.
Daily: drop new files into Inbox; Weekly: empty Inbox and file items into appropriate folders; Archive older items as needed.
Maintain backups with versioning; run duplicate scan periodically.
Weekly cloud sync to a single cloud provider; monthly offline backup to an external drive.
Use only free or built-in tools for cloud sync and backups (no paid software).
Folder hierarchy: six top-level folders: Home/Inbox, Current, Reference, Finance, Photos, Archive.
Minimal useful subfolders: Current/ProjectName; Reference/House, Reference/Medical; Finance/Receipts, Finance/Taxes; Photos/2026 or Photos/2026-02_Event.

Naming pattern: ISO date first; Format: YYYY-MM-DD_shortDescription.ext; Examples: 2026-02-13_cvs_receipt.pdf, 2026-01-20_lease_agreement.pdf, 2025-11-01_insurance_notice.pdf, 2026-03-05_ProjectX_notes_v1.docx; If multiple items same day: add vendor or index (YYYY-MM-DD_vendor_receipt_01.pdf).

Backup checklist: Cloud backup weekly verification; External drive monthly copy (manual or via robocopy/rsync); Verify 1–2 files; Maintain a backup log; Encrypt external drive; Retain cloud + offline copy.
Sample filenames: 2026-02-13_starbucks_receipt_02.pdf, 2026-03-05_ProjectX_expense_report_v1.docx, 2026-04-10_passport_scan.pdf, 2025-12-25_family_photo_scan.jpg

Paper-handling rules (condensed): Create a small physical inbox for incoming paper and process it weekly; Scan every document into Home/Inbox as a searchable PDF, verify the scan, rename the scanned file immediately using YYYY-MM-DD_shortDescription.ext; Recycle originals after successful scan and verification unless the original is legally required; Shred sensitive documents after scanning and verification; Keep originals required by law in a labeled envelope or safe; For receipts, scan and move to Finance/Receipts within your weekly Inbox sweep; Add a short note to the scanned filename or metadata if context is needed; Schedule a weekly 10–15 minute routine: scan, rename, file to Current/Reference/Finance/Photos/Archive, and clear the physical inbox.
Keep originals only as required for legal/tax needs; define retention periods (e.g., 7 years for tax docs).
Limit physical originals to an agreed 'keep originals' list; once full, nothing new goes in until review.
Scan everything else to a searchable PDF and verify scans before recycling originals.
Schedule quarterly/annual 10–15 minute routine to scan, rename, file, and purge.
Offer offsite option for important originals (safe-deposit box or locked home safe).
Scanning reliability: enable auto-rotate in scanning apps and verify orientation/readability before filing.
DPI guidance: default scan resolution should be 300 dpi for documents and receipts; 400 dpi for very small or faint text; 600 dpi only for photos or archival imaging.
Color-coding strategy: limit color-coding to a maximum of 3 colors to reduce upkeep.
Ensure backups are redundant across at least two locations (cloud + encrypted external drive) with versioning and regular verification.
decisions: Establish keep-originals list (examples: passports, birth certificates, property deeds, wills, signed contracts).
Scan everything else to a searchable PDF and verify the scans before recycling the originals.
Keep originals only as required for legal/tax needs; define retention periods (e.g., 7 years for tax docs).
Limit physical storage to one labeled box/envelope; review before adding more.
Schedule quarterly/annual 10–15 minute routine to scan, rename, file, and purge.
Offer offsite storage option for important originals (safe-deposit box or locked home safe).
Default scan resolution policy: 300 dpi for documents/receipts; 400 dpi for very small or faint text; 600 dpi for photos or archival imaging; re-scan if needs arise.
Adopt a 30–60 minute quick receipts triage plan.
Create OriginalsIndex.txt to track kept originals.
Adopt color-coding policy: use a maximum of 3 colors for physical folders and envelopes; color-coding is optional and should be kept minimal.
Adopt client-side encryption for cloud storage of sensitive items using Cryptomator or VeraCrypt.
Enable 2FA on cloud accounts and use selective sync to minimize cloud exposure.
Use encrypted external drives for highly sensitive documents instead of cloud.
Adopt a redundant backup strategy: maintain at least two copies (cloud + encrypted external drive); perform regular restore tests; rotate drives; ensure offsite backup.
open: Define retention periods for non-originals (e.g., 7 years).
Set up offsite storage option decision (safe-deposit box or locked home safe).
Implement physical storage limit (one labeled box) and review workflow.
Adopt a quick verification rule: verify scan orientation immediately after scanning and before filing.
Audit scanning workflow for orientation issues and implement auto-rotation and verification steps.
If hardware misbehaves, adjust scanner settings or consider using a phone scanner as an alternative.
Conduct a 30–60 minute quick triage of receipts to determine which are worth keeping.
Define retention periods for receipts (align with tax/business requirements) per jurisdiction.
Set up a process to scan and file receipts into Finance/Receipts immediately after triage.
Decide on color-coding approach for physical folders; if upkeep proves high, discontinue color-coding.
If zero upkeep is desired, implement a plan to skip color-coding and rely on OriginalsIndex.txt and a single labeled Originals box.
Decide OS and cloud provider to tailor encryption walkthrough.
Set up 2FA and encryption workflow for cloud accounts.
Define and implement redundancy-based backup plan (multi-location backups, restore tests, drive rotation).
Evaluate OCR options for making scanned PDFs searchable; plan to assess user-friendly options when ready.
Implement per-project HomeImprovement receipts workflow for incoming receipts (physical and digital).
Create and update routing/inbox rules to move scanned/received DIY receipts to Home/Finance/Receipts/HomeImprovement/YYYY/ProjectName.
